mnemonics: Always Inorder, Preorder, Postorder (A-I-P-P for traversal types)
keyPoints: Tree insertion follows BST rules: left for smaller, right for larger values.
Three main traversals: Inorder (LNR), Preorder (NLR), Postorder (LRN).
Searching uses BST properties for efficient O(log n) lookups in balanced trees.
Copying a tree requires a full traversal (like postorder) to recreate structure.
commonMistakes: Forgetting to handle the base case (empty tree) in recursive operations.
Confusing traversal orders, especially between inorder and preorder.
Assuming a tree is balanced, leading to incorrect O(log n) operation assumptions.
Shallow copying instead of deep copying, causing shared references.
